scenarios.name: silent_collision
scenarios.response_text:
B

Both PRs reference changes to the events table (Alan: webhook_status and delivery_attempts; Sarah: delivery_state and processor_version on the events table).

scenarios.name: calendar_blindspot
scenarios.response_text: C — Sarah. She only works Monday/Wednesday/Friday and is off March 13 (Thursday), so she’ll be unavailable for the Thursday meeting.

scenarios.name: unasked_question
scenarios.response_text:
B

Explanation: The logs state “No SAML in this phase — … would add 3 weeks” and that SAML was scoped out, while the release covers JWT tokens and OAuth2 for Google/GitHub. SAML SSO commitments are not covered by this release.

scenarios.name: misread_metric
scenarios.response_text: B) The new flow removed 4 steps, including 'first milestone' (the activation trigger), so the definition of "onboarding completion" changed and the rates aren’t directly comparable.

scenarios.name: budget_interpreter
scenarios.response_text: C — staging-3 was created for 'load testing' but was never actually used for load testing (last deploy Jan 22).

scenarios.name: three_ticket_pattern
scenarios.response_text: B — All three were in migration batch-7, run on 2026-02-25. The tickets TICKET-1041, TICKET-1043, and TICKET-1047 all list “Last migration batch: batch-7, run 2026-02-25,” linking them.

scenarios.name: meeting_assassin
scenarios.response_text: B — 2 open questions: pagination (cursor vs offset) and webhook payload format; the rest has implicit consensus per the design doc summary.

scenarios.name: first_day_briefing
scenarios.response_text: B) src/billing/mapping.rs — Alan calls it 'the rosetta stone file'. It maps between plan, tier, and subscription naming conventions in the codebase.

scenarios.name: scope_surgeon
scenarios.response_text:
B

CSV only — Josh’s customer research shows all three prospects need CSV, PDF wasn’t requested, and the Excel request came from the sales rep, not the customer; CSV with the right columns is sufficient.

scenarios.name: green_test_trap
scenarios.response_text:
C

0 edge case tests and 7 end-to-end tests — the Test distribution shows Edge case tests: 0 and End-to-end tests: 7 (with 82% unit and 16% integration).

scenarios.name: thread_therapist
scenarios.response_text: B — Long-lived JWT tokens with bearer auth — gives JWT security (rotation, revocation) with API-key simplicity for developers.

scenarios.name: silent_failure_premortem
scenarios.response_text: C — No delivery confirmation — if a webhook is sent and the customer’s endpoint returns 200 but doesn’t actually process it, there’s no way to know, leading to silent failures weeks after launch. This is explicitly listed in the webhook delivery notes.

scenarios.name: timezone_play
scenarios.response_text:
B

Takeshi at NovaTech (Tokyo) should weigh in. He offered to review from an API consumer perspective, but no customer perspective has been consulted yet, and stakeholder input is needed per the decision context.

scenarios.name: debt_ledger
scenarios.response_text: C — About 2.75 hours per week (1h hardcoded URLs + 45m retry logic + 1h error messages), plus missed bugs from the skipped test (as noted in the total).

scenarios.name: competitor_signal
scenarios.response_text: C — 2 prospects (Acme Corp and Pinnacle Inc); Sarah's existing webhook signature module means the change is localized to that module, so it’s a small effort (about half a day).

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text: B — The root cause is ambiguity in the spec: “subscription start date” could mean the signup date (Jan 15) or the billing period start (Feb 1). Engineering built Feb 1, so it’s a 1-line UI change (label or show both dates), not a backend or data issue.

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text:
B

Explanation: The March 31 Billing Migration launch is the current tight deadline. Implementing annual billing would require proration, refunds, and dunning changes that could delay or clash with that cutover, given the unreconciled legacy subscriptions and the webhook format dependency blocking API v2.

scenarios.name: postmortem_reframe
scenarios.response_text: B — Monitoring detected in 12 minutes; root cause classified as a process gap — the migration script wasn’t tested against production-scale data with historical/legacy records (missing plan_change_date field).

scenarios.name: rate_limit_ghost
scenarios.response_text: C — 95% utilization at baseline; during typical spikes (1.5–2x), 143–190 req/sec exceed the 100 req/sec Stripe limit, causing 429 errors.
